Clinician's Notes: Lumbar spine physiological curvature is decreased. The lumbar vertebral bodies have signals within normal limits. No compression or spondylolisthesis is seen. Signal decrease on T2W images is seen due to degeneration. Annular tear of the L3/L4 and L4/L5 intervertebral discs. Hypertrophy of the ligamentum flavum from L3 to L5 and bulging of the lumbar intervertebral discs causing spinal stenosis, with the narrowest anterior-posterior diameter of approximately 5.5mm (at the L4/L5 level), resulting in compression of the L4, L5, and S1 nerve roots bilaterally in the lateral recess and the L5 nerve roots bilaterally in the intervertebral foramina. No abnormality is seen in the size and signal of the conus medullaris. No abnormality is seen in the soft tissues surrounding the vertebral bodies. No abnormality is seen in the sacroiliac joints bilaterally. Minimal joint effusion is seen at the L2/L3, L3/L4, L4/L5, and L5/S1 facet joints bilaterally, with the maximum thickness of approximately 2.5mm.
